pi_items.source: GPS coordinates from latitude.to/articles-by-country/us/united-states/17021/wallops-flight-facility confirm Wallops Flight Facility latitude as 37.9402°N. Wikipedia and NASA sources corroborate ~37.94°N. The NOAA WCDA station is co-located at the Wallops Island complex.

Wallops Flight Facility longitude is approximately -75.46°W per multiple sources. GPS coordinate databases show -75.4664 for the main WFF facility; -75.4576 corresponds to the NOAA CDA antenna farm area, ~770m east. Both are within the facility footprint and the difference is negligible for satellite pass prediction (<0.05s timing difference). ICAO KWAL airport reference point is at similar longitude.

AirNav KWAL reports field elevation of 40 feet (12.2m). NDACC Wallops station lists 13m. The CDA facility antennas sit on slightly higher ground within the complex. 15m is a reasonable approximation; the difference from 12-13m is negligible for satellite tracking (changes elevation angles by <0.001 degrees). The actual .qth file in the setup uses ALT=15.

ICAO code KWAL confirmed by: AirNav (airnav.com/airport/KWAL), METAR services (metar-taf.com/metar/KWAL), Weather Underground (wunderground.com/weather/KWAL), FlightAware, NOAA forecast.weather.gov, and AOPA. KWAL is the Wallops Flight Facility airport weather station, the closest meteorological reporting station to the NOAA CDA facility.

All six NORAD catalog numbers independently verified via n2yo.com, SatNOGS DB, and CelesTrak: NOAA-15=25338 (launched 1998, sun-synchronous 807km), NOAA-18=28654 (launched 2005, 854km), NOAA-19=33591 (launched 2009), METOP-B=38771 (launched 2012, ESA/EUMETSAT), METOP-C=43689 (launched 2018-087A, ESA/EUMETSAT), SUOMI NPP=37849 (launched 2011, NASA/NOAA joint). All are confirmed operational polar-orbiting weather/environmental satellites in the POES/EPS/JPSS constellations. All six .sat files exist in the GPredict satdata directory with valid TLE data (verified in live environment).

ISS (ZARYA)=25544 confirmed via SatNOGS DB and n2yo.com (International Space Station, 1998-067A). HST=20580 confirmed via n2yo.com (Hubble Space Telescope). NORAD 28474 confirmed as GPS BIIR-13 (PRN 02) via in-the-sky.org and CelesTrak GPS operational group — note: GPredict displays this as 'GPS BIIR-13 (PRN 02)', not 'GPS BIIR-2' as stated in the task description; however the NORAD ID is correct and it is clearly a GPS navigation satellite, not a POES weather satellite. All three .sat files confirmed present in the live environment satdata directory.

GPredict v2.2 source code (save-pass.c, pass-to-txt.c) confirms the 'Upcoming passes' dialog has a '_Save' button (RESPONSE_SAVE=11) that calls save_passes() to write a text file with pass prediction table. Default filename pattern is '<satname>-passes.txt'. The save dialog allows choosing folder and filename. ~/Documents/ directory confirmed to exist with correct ownership (ga:ga) in the live environment. The agent can navigate the file chooser to this path.

privileged_info_summary: The Wallops_CDA ground station should have latitude 37.9402°N, longitude 75.4576°W (stored as -75.4576 in GPredict, negative means West), altitude 15m, and ICAO weather station code KWAL. The initial setup has three deliberate errors: latitude is 73.9402 (digits 3 and 7 swapped), longitude is stored as positive 75.4576 (meaning East instead of West), and weather code is KWFF instead of KWAL. The default ground station should be changed from Pittsburgh.qth to Wallops_CDA.qth. The POES_Tracking module initially contains ISS/ZARYA (NORAD 25544), HST/Hubble (NORAD 20580), and GPS BIIR-13/PRN 02 (NORAD 28474) — all wrong. The correct POES fleet satellites are: NOAA 15 (25338), NOAA 18 (28654), NOAA 19 (33591), METOP-B (38771), METOP-C (43689), and SUOMI NPP (37849). All six have .sat data files pre-installed in the GPredict satdata directory. The module should use Wallops_CDA.qth as its ground station binding. GPredict's preferences should be set to Metric units (USE_IMPERIAL=false) and UTC time (USE_LOCAL_TIME=false). The module layout should be 'Map and Table' (also called 'World map and table') with the topographical map background (nasa-topo files available at /usr/share/gpredict/data/maps/) and solar terminator line enabled. The satellite table should show Azimuth, Elevation, Range, and Range Rate columns. GPredict's pass prediction save feature (accessible via right-click on satellite → Future Passes → Save button) writes a text file containing pass data with AOS/TCA/LOS times. The pass prediction file for METOP-C should be saved to /home/ga/Documents/METOP_C_passes.txt. Note: GPredict uses [QTH] section header for .qth files and [GLOBAL] section header for .mod files. The config keys for preferences are USE_IMPERIAL (not unit=) and USE_LOCAL_TIME (not TFORMAT=) under [GLOBAL] in gpredict.cfg.
